text.title: Google Display Network Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-display-ads methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every Display campaign MUST stack ≥3 of {in-market, affinity, custom intent, remarketing}.
rule.rationale: Single-audience Display under-targets; layering 3+ tiers controls CPC × audience-fit.

rule.statement: Each campaign MUST exclude mobile apps by default and apply the standard low-quality-site placement exclusion list.
rule.rationale: Mobile apps + parked-domain sites consume 30-50% of Display budget at near-zero CTR.

rule.statement: Creative set MUST include responsive display + native + image (≥3 sizes: 300x250, 728x90, 320x50).
rule.rationale: Coverage across surfaces; missing native loses 30% inventory.

rule.statement: Frequency cap MUST be set per campaign: 5/day, 15/week. Above that creative fatigues fast.
rule.rationale: Display reach is broad; uncapped frequency hits 30+ daily on news / video sites.

rule.statement: Display reports MUST track both CPA ceiling (upper-funnel attribution) AND CPM floor (cost-of-reach benchmark).
rule.rationale: Judging Display on Search-CPA alone undervalues awareness; CPM alone hides waste.
